mnemonics: Dual = Flip Roles: Think of a map and its countries. The dual swaps faces for vertices. A face in the original graph becomes a vertex in the dual, and vice-versa for a vertex inside a face.
keyPoints: A geometric dual is a graph obtained by placing a vertex inside each face of the original planar graph.
An edge is drawn in the dual for every edge in the original graph, connecting the vertices of the faces it separated.
The dual graph is also planar, and the original graph is the dual of its dual (if the graph is connected).
commonMistakes: Assuming every graph has a geometric dual (only planar graphs do).
Confusing geometric duality with other types of graph duality (e.g., abstract duality).
Forgetting that a cutset in the original graph corresponds to a cycle in its dual, and vice-versa.
